skill.name: avalonia-viewmodels-zafiro
skill.description: Best practices and patterns for creating ViewModels, Wizards, and navigation in Avalonia applications using Zafiro and ReactiveUI.
skill.summary: A comprehensive guide for building reactive Avalonia UI applications with Zafiro toolkit patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 79 potential issues (71 external_commands, 8 blocker patterns). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The detected 'backtick execution' patterns are markdown code fence markers (```) and C# attribute syntax ([Section], [Reactive]). The 'blocker' patterns are documentation callouts ([!TIP], [!IMPORTANT]). This skill contains only documentation markdown files with C# code examples - no executable code or security risks exist.

content.user_title: Build reactive Avalonia UI applications with Zafiro patterns
content.value_statement: Creating maintainable Avalonia applications requires consistent ViewModel patterns and navigation structures. This skill provides proven ReactiveUI and Zafiro toolkit patterns for robust UI development.
actual_capabilities: Generate reactive ViewModels using ReactiveObject base class with source generators
Create multi-step wizards using SlimWizard and WizardBuilder patterns
Implement section-based navigation with automatic [Section] attribute discovery
Design enhanced commands with IEnhancedCommand for progress reporting
Set up dependency injection using CompositionRoot patterns
limitations: Requires Avalonia UI framework and Zafiro toolkit as dependencies
Patterns assume familiarity with ReactiveUI and functional-reactive programming
Source generators require compatible C# compiler and build configuration
Does not provide ready-made components, only architectural patterns and guidance

use_cases.title: Desktop application with modular sections
use_cases.description: Build a wallet application with automatically discovered sections like Wallet, Browse, and Settings using [Section] attributes and INavigator for seamless navigation between views.
use_cases.target_user: .NET developers building cross-platform desktop applications with Avalonia UI

use_cases.title: Complex multi-step workflow implementation
use_cases.description: Create project setup wizards with validation, progress tracking, and conditional navigation using WizardBuilder to define steps with WhenValid and NextCommand rules.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers implementing onboarding flows or configuration wizards in enterprise applications

use_cases.title: Reactive command infrastructure setup
use_cases.description: Establish robust command patterns with error handling via HandleErrorsWith, automatic disposables management with CompositeDisposable, and reactive property observation using WhenAnyValue.
use_cases.target_user: MVVM developers seeking consistent patterns for command execution and state management

prompt_templates.title: Basic ViewModel creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ReactiveUI ViewModel for a user profile screen with Name, Email, and IsBusy properties. Include a Save command that validates the email format before execution. Use [Reactive] attributes and proper disposable handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Creating your first reactive ViewModel with basic properties and commands

prompt_templates.title: Section-based navigation setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to implement a sidebar navigation with three sections: Dashboard, Reports, and Settings. Use the [Section] attribute with FontAwesome icons and demonstrate how to switch between sections using IShellViewModel.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Setting up modular UI sections with automatic discovery

prompt_templates.title: Multi-step wizard with validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a wizard for creating a new project with four steps: Project Info, Select Template, Configure Settings, and Review. Each step must validate before proceeding. Use WizardBuilder with WhenValid rules and show how to handle the final result.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Implementing complex wizard flows with conditional navigation

prompt_templates.title: Enhanced command with error handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Demonstrate creating an IEnhancedCommand for data synchronization that shows progress, handles errors through NotificationService, and properly disposes subscriptions. Include cancellation support and reactive enable/disable logic based on connection state.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert - Production-ready command patterns with full error handling and progress reporting

output_examples.input: Generate a ViewModel for a login screen with username and password fields
output_examples.output: A partial class LoginViewModel inheriting from ReactiveObject with [Reactive] attributes for Username and Password properties, a LoginCommand created via ReactiveCommand.CreateFromTask with validation, and proper IDisposable implementation with CompositeDisposable for cleanup.

output_examples.input: How do I navigate from HomeViewModel to DetailsViewModel when a user clicks an item?
output_examples.output: Inject INavigator into HomeViewModel constructor, then call await navigator.Navigate(() => new DetailsViewModel(selectedItem)) in your selection handler. The DataTypeViewLocator automatically resolves the corresponding DetailsView.

best_practices: Always use .DisposeWith(disposables) on subscriptions and commands to prevent memory leaks in reactive streams
Mark section ViewModels with [Section] attribute for automatic registration instead of manual wiring in CompositionRoot
Use HandleErrorsWith extension to channel command errors to NotificationService for consistent user feedback
anti_patterns: Do not manually instantiate ViewModels with 'new' outside of factory methods - use dependency injection through CompositionRoot
Avoid subscribing to observables without adding them to CompositeDisposable - this causes memory leaks
Never block async operations in command execution - always use CreateFromTask and await navigation calls

faq.question: What dependencies do I need to use these patterns?
faq.answer: You need Avalonia UI, ReactiveUI, ReactiveUI.SourceGenerators, and the Zafiro toolkit. Add them via NuGet packages to your project file.

faq.question: How does automatic section discovery work?
faq.answer: The [Section] attribute marks ViewModels as sections. Zafiro's AddSectionsFromAttributes scans assemblies at startup and registers marked types with the dependency injection container.

faq.question: Can I use these patterns without the full Zafiro toolkit?
faq.answer: Yes, the core ReactiveUI patterns work independently. However, SlimWizard, IEnhancedCommand, and section discovery require Zafiro components.

faq.question: How do I handle validation in wizard steps?
faq.answer: Implement validation in your step ViewModel using WhenAnyValue to observe property changes and set a validity flag. Use .WhenValid() in the WizardBuilder to block navigation until validation passes.

faq.question: What is the purpose of IEnhancedCommand over standard ICommand?
faq.answer: IEnhancedCommand extends ICommand with Name and Text metadata for UI binding, plus built-in progress reporting and error handling integration with NotificationService.

faq.question: How do I test ViewModels created with these patterns?
faq.answer: ViewModels are plain classes with injectable dependencies. Use moq or NSubstitute for mocks, and test reactive properties using ReactiveUI.Testing or by observing property changes directly.
